tagline: One prompt, many AI chats, one side panel.
manifest_description: Browser-native AI compare workspace for ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Qwen, and Grok in one browser side panel.
repo_description: Compare-first, browser-native AI compare workspace for ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Qwen, and Grok, with governed coding-agent integrations for Codex and Claude Code browser workflows.
landing_title: Prompt Switchboard | Compare-first browser AI workspace
landing_description: Prompt Switchboard is a compare-first, browser-native AI compare workspace for ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Qwen, and Grok in one browser side panel, with governed coding-agent integrations for Codex and Claude Code browser workflows.
landing_og_description: Ask once, compare ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Qwen, and Grok side by side, keep the trust boundary in your browser, and move from install to first compare without tab juggling.
current_install_surface: The supported install path today is the packaged GitHub Release zip.
store_ready_note: Browser-store submission materials are being kept ready, but GitHub Releases remains the supported install surface today.
official_registry_live_note: The official MCP Registry already returns a live Prompt Switchboard MCP entry for the same governed integration surface.
browser_store_public_note: The browser-store install path is still not live today, and the public Chrome surface does not yet provide an exact Prompt Switchboard listing proof.
marketing_alias_note: Future .ai or campaign domains should redirect to the canonical Prompt Switchboard homepage instead of replacing the primary product brand.
analysis_lane_note: Optional AI analysis can review a compare turn through a browser tab you already trust or, when available, through a separate local Switchyard runtime. It summarizes consensus and disagreement, recommends the best-fit answer to continue from, and drafts the next question as decision guidance without replacing the core compare-first lane. Direct browser API-key execution stays gated in this build.
mcp_integration_note: The governed MCP integration exposes Prompt Switchboard product actions over stdio and a localhost bridge. OpenAI Codex and Claude Code are the strongest repo-specific bindings for that surface. This repo also ships public bundle-ready MCP packets for OpenCode and OpenClaw plus a Docker wrapper for the same integration path, but those lanes remain narrower or unverified host paths until Prompt Switchboard ships a tested binding and an official published listing of its own. The integration does not expose arbitrary browser automation or a hosted relay.
workflow_lane_note: The built-in next-step workflow keeps seed-only actions distinct from real compare execution. Workflow snapshots remain session-scoped runtime cache instead of durable cold-start truth, and the workflow tool surface only supports run/list/get/resume inside the current browser session.
operator_helper_note: The repo-local operator helper wraps doctor, server, smoke, status, readiness, and workflow inspection for maintainers. It is not a public CLI product surface.
docker_integration_note: The repo now also ships a Docker entry for the governed MCP integration and operator helper. That container surface is useful for coding-host integration work, but it is not a hosted compare service, a public HTTP API, or proof of a Glama listing.
social_preview_alt: Prompt Switchboard social preview showing the compare-first browser extension.
social_preview_manual_check: Manually confirm the live GitHub Social Preview image whenever front-door visuals change.
